handoffConclusion: DevView's roadmap is complete to the intended safe MVP boundary for this calibration: natural-language frontend candidate handling reaches instruction pack preview through deterministic graph/slice/contract stages; advisory backend report/proposal/review surfaces exist; analyzer and clarification are candidate-only; project-specific extension readiness now represents declarative Project Profile and Extension Manifest validation without executing extension code; Hook Gateway activation is represented through non-active previews and repo-local advisory script bundle materialization; approved apply dry-run, guarded apply reporting, Evidence decision/accepted Evidence, Runtime Evidence Satisfaction readiness, Equivalence readiness, and Scope/CI readiness are represented while runtime satisfaction, equivalence proof, enforcement, hook activation, extension execution, approval automation, and user acceptance automation remain disabled.
safeEndStateMeaning: Reached means the control/compiler path is represented and reviewable without granting execution, approval, graph mutation, Evidence acceptance, equivalence proof, scope enforcement, CI enforcement, or hook activation authority.

handoffLanes.start: Natural Language Request / Request IR Candidate
handoffLanes.requiredNextHumanAction: Review Instruction Pack before any Codex execution.
handoffLanes.authorityBoundary: Instruction Pack preview is not Codex execution and cannot approve or mutate graph-source.

handoffLanes.requiredNextHumanAction: Use external or future analyzer output only as Request IR Candidate input, then rerun validation before traversal.
handoffLanes.authorityBoundary: No LLM provider is implemented; external candidate import remains candidate-only.

handoffLanes.requiredNextHumanAction: Review declarative project profile and extension manifest capabilities before designing controlled adapters.
handoffLanes.authorityBoundary: Extension manifests are declarative only; no extension code, provider, network, shell, mutation, runtime satisfaction, equivalence, or enforcement authority is granted.

handoffLanes.requiredNextHumanAction: Decide separately whether to design an explicit hook install/trust flow.
handoffLanes.authorityBoundary: Repo-local advisory scripts are review artifacts only; hooks are not installed, active, trusted, or blocking.

handoffLanes.requiredNextHumanAction: Human review decides whether to defer, request revision, or future-approve a proposal.
handoffLanes.authorityBoundary: Proposal/review packet generation does not apply graph deltas or mutate graph-source.

handoffLanes.requiredNextHumanAction: Resolve the runtime Evidence obligation mismatch and design future runtime satisfaction/equivalence/enforcement lifecycles before any enforcement path.
handoffLanes.authorityBoundary: Approved apply dry-run and accepted Evidence are represented, but the current Todo graph apply report is blocked-no-concrete-operations, runtime Evidence satisfaction readiness is blocked by obligation mismatch, Equivalence readiness is blocked by runtime satisfaction readiness, and Scope/CI readiness is blocked and non-enforcing.

doNextIfContinuingRoadmap.workItem: Broaden fixtures and external project coverage
doNextIfContinuingRoadmap.reason: The safe MVP path is complete for the Todo App calibration but not product-generalized across graph/source shapes.
doNextIfContinuingRoadmap.forbiddenShortcut: Do not use broader fixture coverage to claim automatic approval, graph-source mutation, or enforcement.

doNextIfContinuingRoadmap.reason: Activation previews and repo-local script bundle exist, but active hooks are intentionally not installed or trusted.
doNextIfContinuingRoadmap.forbiddenShortcut: Do not mutate Codex config, install hooks, enable guided/strict blocking, or trust repositories without an explicit human decision boundary.

doNextIfContinuingRoadmap.workItem: Design actual LLM analyzer provider integration
doNextIfContinuingRoadmap.reason: Analyzer pack and provider-disabled import surface exist; actual provider execution remains future.
doNextIfContinuingRoadmap.forbiddenShortcut: Do not allow unvalidated analyzer output to drive traversal, selected slice, contract input, instruction pack generation, or Codex execution.

doNextIfContinuingRoadmap.reason: Project profile and extension manifest readiness are represented, but adapters remain declarative and non-executing until a permission, audit, and RBAC model is designed.
doNextIfContinuingRoadmap.forbiddenShortcut: Do not execute extension code, call providers, make network calls, run shell commands, mutate graph-source, or let extension manifests grant policy authority.

doNextIfContinuingRoadmap.workItem: Design runtime satisfaction, equivalence, and enforcement lifecycles after the accepted Evidence boundary
doNextIfContinuingRoadmap.reason: Phase 13 is connected through approved apply dry-run and accepted Evidence, but runtime satisfaction readiness is blocked by obligation mismatch and downstream proof/enforcement remain disabled.
doNextIfContinuingRoadmap.forbiddenShortcut: Do not apply graph deltas, mutate graph-source, satisfy runtime Evidence, prove equivalence, enforce scope, configure CI, require checks, change branch protection, or reject diffs from current readiness previews.

explicitlyStillDisabled: graph-source mutation from the current Todo calibration

verificationSnapshot.lastKnownCommit: cd8082e Repoint Scope CI enforcement readiness to runtime-gated equivalence

nonExecutionBoundary: This final handoff preview summarizes roadmap readiness only. It does not execute Codex or extension code, call an LLM/API/provider, make network calls, run shell commands, activate hooks, record new human decisions, approve proposals, apply graph deltas, mutate graph-source, accept Evidence, satisfy runtime Evidence, prove equivalence, enforce scope, configure CI, configure required checks, change branch protection, reject diffs, or replace user acceptance.
